- CPS - construction professional services
- Management, surveillance and quality assurance
- Monitoring during construction of a new bridge
- life of structures. For concrete structures, material properties change through ageing. It is impor-tant
- to know whether or not the required values are achieved and maintained. Defects (e.g. pre-mature
- cracking) that arise during construction may have serious consequences on structural
- performance. Monitoring data helps engineers to understand the real behavior of the structure
- Important information obtained through monitoring during construction includes the following:
- Estimation of hardening time of concrete in order to estimate when shrinkage stresses begin to
- be generated; Deformation measurements during early age of concrete in order to estima

_Captured Friday, 28 July 2017 10:39 a.m. · General › Consents & Practice_

- The following Witness and Hold points shall be incorporated into the Constructor’s Inspection and Test Plan (ITP) for the bridge.
- Witness Points
- The Constructor shall give the Designer 24 hours’ notice and allow the Designer to perform an adequate inspection before commencing the operations below.
- All reinforcing prior to concrete pours or covering over (eg with formwork) including prefabricated elements
- Installation of precast beams
- Installation of precast elements
- All compressible filler board prior to covering over or pouring concrete
- Inspection of shear keys after galvanising but prior to installing denso tape
- All formwork prior to placement of reinforcing and/ or concrete
- Grouting of shear keys into precast beams
- All joints in deck prior to placing surfacing
- Hold Points
- The Constructor shall not proceed beyond a Hold Point until the Designer has expressly released the Hold Point in writing.
- The Designer shall be given 7 days’ notice to allow review of proposals prior to commencing the following work elements. These works shall not be progressed without prior approval from th

_Captured Friday, 3 August 2018 10:43 a.m. · General › Materials & MSQA_

- DA879 ( QA5019HEB2)
- Query
- We note that the cement content is higher than allowed for in the project specifications. Please confirm with the fabricator that this mix design has been used previously for this application with no adverse effects in terms of workability, rate of strength gains, and subsequent dimensional change of units. If they haven’t used it before, it may need to be adjusted after the initial casting to achieve appropriate rate of strength development.
- Response
- The precast fabricator has confirmed this concrete mix
- QA5019HEB2 is the standard mix used daily in the Precast yard with no adverse effects on workability, strength gains, nor has there been any issues with shrinkage.
- The appropriate/designed transfer strengths with be achieved for all items cast using this mix.

- Unlike cement grout which is made from a cementitious powder mix , epoxy grout is made from epoxy resins and a filler powder. Epoxy grout is extremely durable and almost completely stain proof.
- Regular cement grout is not waterproof, so unlike epoxy grout , it can absorb water when wet and stain easily.

_Captured Thursday, 17 June 2021 3:52 PM · General › Bearings & Joints_

- Sika
- SikaSwell profiles can be used in combination with precast panels, some standard details are attached. The key thing is that SikaSwell must be encapsulated with concrete or grout. Minimum cover requirements are outlined on the Product Data Sheets. They work by swelling against concrete to form a gasket type seal when in contact with water. If concrete cover is insufficient the consequent pressure can crack concrete causing spalling.
- We see two potential issues with the detailed joint seal.
- Potential incompatibility with bitumen.
- Insufficient confinement – the SikaSwell will simply swell to displace and fill surrounding voids. It will be ineffective at sealing.
- Sikadur Combiflex SG system could be applied on the inside of the culvert as a joint sealing system. Cover plates would be needed to protect from traffic and support from potential hydrostatic pressure from behind (images below).
- Sika Emseal DSM-DS system mayalso be a good joint sealing solution in this case. Cover plates for traffic protection may be advisable – will confirm.
- Emseal is made to measure and sourced from USA.
- Lead ti

- For SH36 pedestrian bridge Project
- We have reviewed the mix design provided in NTE008 and following are our comments. In addition, as the question about concrete testing was raised in the meeting on 30 Oct 2018, the following provides some clarification of the requirement:
- Please provide mix design 402 and 402P for our information as required by Clause 2.10.3 of NZS 3104.
- In general, we are happy with the mix design 402T, however, please provide water content or W/C ratio for our information.
- It is not acceptable to use concrete mix 401T for the piles. As required by section 2.1.4.1 of “TC21/18 SH36 Pedestrian Overbridge – Structural Concrete Technical Specification” cast in-situ concrete shall have coarse aggregate particles of at least 19mm nominal size.
- For the pile concrete, if it is placed underwater:
- The concrete placed underwater shall be considered as special concrete and should satisfy the requirements of the appendix B of the “
- T-CES 102 (NZ): Specification for Special Concrete in General Civil Engineering Works”
- .
- Slump test shall be taken at the site immediately prior to pla
